name: Cell-type-targeted mitochondrial transplantation rescued degeneration models
summary: MitoCatch improves the technology-readiness boundary by showing targeted mitochondrial delivery across human and mouse cell systems plus mouse injury-model rescue signals.
statement: A binder-based MitoCatch platform targeted donor mitochondria to selected human and mouse cell types, supported mitochondrial internalization and intracellular dynamics, and promoted survival in damaged-neuron models in vitro and in mice.
population_or_model: Human and mouse cell systems plus mouse neuronal injury models
quantitative_note: The abstract reports tunable delivery efficiency through engineered binder affinities but does not provide aging efficacy outcomes.
caveats: The study is delivery-platform and disease-model evidence, not an older-adult or healthy-aging intervention trial.
Human relevance is mostly cell-system evidence, not treated-human evidence.
The platform does not resolve long-term in vivo persistence, immune response, off-target biodistribution, or manufacturing scalability.
The in vivo rescue model is injury and degeneration context rather than normal aging.
